2603.28251.title: DiffAttn: Diffusion-Based Drivers' Visual Attention Prediction with LLM-Enhanced Semantic Reasoning
2603.28251.paper_summary: Drivers' visual attention provides critical cues for anticipating latent hazards and directly shapes decision-making and control maneuvers, where its absence can compromise traffic safety. To emulate drivers' perception patterns and advance visual attention prediction for intelligent vehicles, we propose DiffAttn, a diffusion-based framework that formulates this task as a conditional diffusion-denoising process, enabling more accurate modeling of drivers' attention. To capture both local and global scene features, we adopt Swin Transformer as encoder and design a decoder that combines a Feature Fusion Pyramid for cross-layer interaction with dense, multi-scale conditional diffusion to jointly enhance denoising learning and model fine-grained local and global scene contexts. Additionally, a large language model (LLM) layer is incorporated to enhance top-down semantic reasoning and improve sensitivity to safety-critical cues. Extensive experiments on four public datasets demonstrate that DiffAttn achieves state-of-the-art (SoTA) performance, surpassing most video-based, top-down-feature-driven, and LLM-enhanced baselines. Our framework further supports interpretable driver-centric scene understanding and has the potential to improve in-cabin human-machine interaction, risk perception, and drivers' state measurement in intelligent vehicles.
2603.28251.paper_summary_zh: 駕駛者的視覺注意力提供了預測潛在危險的關鍵線索，並直接影響決策和控制操作，缺乏這種注意力可能會危害交通安全。為了模擬駕駛者的感知模式並推進智能車輛的視覺注意力預測，我們提出了DiffAttn，一個基於擴散的框架，將這一任務表述為條件擴散去噪過程，從而實現對駕駛者注意力的更準確建模。為了捕捉局部和全局場景特徵，我們採用Swin Transformer作為編碼器，並設計了一個解碼器，結合了特徵融合金字塔以實現跨層交互，並使用密集的多尺度條件擴散來共同增強去噪學習和模型的細緻局部及全局場景上下文。此外，還加入了一個大型語言模型（LLM）層，以增強自上而下的語義推理並提高對安全關鍵線索的敏感性。在四個公共數據集上的大量實驗表明，DiffAttn實現了最先進的（SoTA）性能，超越了大多數基於視頻的、自上而下特徵驅動的和LLM增強的基準。我們的框架進一步支持可解釋的以駕駛者為中心的場景理解，並具有改善智能車輛內部人機互動、風險感知和駕駛者狀態測量的潛力。
2603.28251.author: Weimin Liu et.al.
2603.28251.authors: Weimin Liu, Qingkun Li, Jiyuan Qiu, Wenjun Wang, Joshua H. Meng
